rule.hazard: unassessed harms to people, organizations, and society
conditions: early in the system's development
when defining the product vision and requirements
requirements: Complete an Impact Assessment that assesses the system's impact on people, organizations, and society.

rule.hazard: unassessed harms to people, organizations, and society
conditions: early in the system's development
when defining the product vision and requirements
requirements: Document the Impact Assessment effort using the Impact Assessment template provided by the Office of Responsible AI.

rule.hazard: development without reviewed impact assessment
conditions: with the reviewers identified according to your organization's compliance process
before development starts
requirements: developer shall review the completed Impact Assessment with the reviewers identified according to your organization's compliance process before development starts.

rule.hazard: development proceeding without required approvals
conditions: from the reviewers identified according to your organization's compliance process
requirements: developer shall secure all required approvals from the reviewers identified according to your organization's compliance process.

rule.hazard: outdated impact assessment
conditions: at least annually
requirements: The org must update the Impact Assessment at least annually.

rule.hazard: outdated impact assessment
conditions: at least annually
requirements: The org must review the Impact Assessment at least annually.

rule.hazard: outdated impact assessment
conditions: when new intended uses are added
requirements: The org must update the Impact Assessment when new intended uses are added.

rule.hazard: outdated impact assessment
conditions: when new intended uses are added
requirements: The org must review the Impact Assessment when new intended uses are added.

rule.hazard: outdated impact assessment
conditions: before advancing to a new release stage
requirements: The org must update the Impact Assessment before advancing to a new release stage.

rule.hazard: outdated impact assessment
conditions: before advancing to a new release stage
requirements: The org must review the Impact Assessment before advancing to a new release stage.

rule.hazard: unidentified Restricted Use system leading to unmitigated significant adverse impacts
conditions: Applies to: All AI systems
requirements: org must review the defined Restricted Uses to determine whether the system meets the definition of any Restricted Use.

rule.hazard: unidentified Restricted Use system leading to unmitigated significant adverse impacts
conditions: system meets the definition of any Restricted Use
requirements: org must document in the Impact Assessment when the system meets the definition of any Restricted Use.

rule.hazard: unidentified Restricted Use system leading to unmitigated significant adverse impacts
conditions: system meets the definition of any Restricted Use
requirements: org must follow the requirements for the Restricted Use when the system meets the definition of any Restricted Use.

rule.hazard: failure to identify Sensitive Use
requirements: Answer prompts in the Impact Assessment template to determine whether the system meets the definition of a Sensitive Use.

rule.hazard: unreported Sensitive Use
conditions: system meets the definition of a Sensitive Use
requirements: Report the system to the Office of Responsible AI.

rule.hazard: non-compliance with Sensitive Uses review
conditions: system meets the definition of a Sensitive Use
requirements: Follow any additional requirements resulting from a Sensitive Uses review.

rule.hazard: failure to identify systems that meet the criteria for Sensitive or Restricted Uses
conditions: at least annually
requirements: org shall review systems against the definitions for Sensitive Uses and Restricted Uses at least annually.

rule.hazard: undetected deployment of systems that qualify as Sensitive Uses
conditions: systems meet the criteria for Sensitive Uses
requirements: org shall report systems that meet the criteria for Sensitive Uses to the Office of Responsible AI.

rule.hazard: undetected deployment of systems that qualify as Restricted Uses
conditions: systems meet the criteria for Restricted Uses
requirements: org shall notify the Office of Responsible AI of systems that meet the criteria for Restricted Uses.

rule.hazard: system not solving intended problem
conditions: for each intended use
requirements: org shall document in the Impact Assessment how the system's use will solve the problem posed by each intended use.

rule.hazard: inappropriate use due to undocumented model limitations
conditions: for each model in the AI system
requirements: Developer shall define and document for each model the model's proposed inputs and analyze how well those inputs represent the concepts they are intended to represent, including the limitations of this representation.

rule.hazard: inappropriate use due to undocumented model limitations
conditions: for each model in the AI system
requirements: Developer shall define and document for each model the model's proposed outputs and analyze how well those outputs represent the concepts they are intended to represent, including the limitations of this representation.

rule.hazard: inappropriate use due to undocumented model limitations
conditions: for each model in the AI system
requirements: Developer shall define and document limitations to the generalizability of each resulting model based on the training and testing data that will be used.

rule.hazard: inappropriate use due to undocumented model limitations
conditions: for this Goal
requirements: Developer shall define and document Responsible Release Criteria for this Goal.

rule.hazard: inappropriate use due to undocumented model limitations
conditions: for this Goal
requirements: Developer shall document a concise definition of the problem being solved in the intended use as part of the Responsible Release Criteria.

rule.hazard: inappropriate use due to undocumented model limitations
conditions: for this Goal
requirements: Developer shall document the performance metrics and their Responsible Release Criteria and the error types and their Responsible Release Criteria as part of the Responsible Release Criteria.

rule.hazard: inappropriate use due to undocumented model limitations
conditions: for each of the performance metrics and error types
requirements: Developer shall document an evaluation plan for each of the performance metrics and error types.

rule.hazard: undetected model issues due to missing or undocumented evaluations
requirements: developer shall use the methods defined in requirement A3.4 to conduct evaluations.

rule.hazard: undetected model issues due to missing or undocumented evaluations
requirements: developer shall document the pre-release results of the evaluations.

rule.hazard: undetected model issues due to missing or undocumented evaluations
requirements: developer shall determine and document how often ongoing evaluation should be conducted to continue supporting this Goal.

rule.hazard: use without evidence of fitness for purpose
requirements: Provide documentation to customers that describes evidence that the system is fit for purpose for each intended use.

rule.hazard: use for unintended purposes
requirements: Provide documentation to customers that describes the system's intended uses.

rule.hazard: insufficient transparency for platform customers
conditions: system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners
requirements: When the system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners, include the documentation describing intended uses and evidence of fitness for purpose in the required Transparency Note.

rule.hazard: action based on unsupported intended use
conditions: If an intended use is not supported by evidence, or if evidence comes to light that refutes that the system is fit for purpose for the intended use at any point in the system's use
requirements: org shall remove the intended use from customer-facing materials and make current customers aware of the issue.

rule.hazard: action based on unsupported intended use
conditions: If an intended use is not supported by evidence, or if evidence comes to light that refutes that the system is fit for purpose for the intended use at any point in the system's use
requirements: org shall take action to close the identified gap or discontinue the system.

rule.hazard: action based on unsupported intended use
conditions: If an intended use is not supported by evidence, or if evidence comes to light that refutes that the system is fit for purpose for the intended use at any point in the system's use
requirements: org shall revise documentation related to the intended use.

rule.hazard: action based on unsupported intended use
conditions: If an intended use is not supported by evidence, or if evidence comes to light that refutes that the system is fit for purpose for the intended use at any point in the system's use
requirements: org shall publish the revised documentation to customers.

rule.hazard: action based on unsupported intended use
conditions: If an intended use is not supported by evidence, or if evidence comes to light that refutes that the system is fit for purpose for the intended use at any point in the system's use
When the system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners
requirements: org shall include this information in the required Transparency Note.

rule.hazard: use of data unsuitable for intended uses, stakeholders, or deployment geography
requirements: Define and document data requirements with respect to the system's intended uses, stakeholders, and the geographic areas where the system will be deployed.

rule.hazard: use of data unsuitable for intended uses, stakeholders, or deployment geography
requirements: Document the defined data requirements in the Impact Assessment.

rule.hazard: poor data quality from inadequate data processing
requirements: org shall define and document procedures for the collection and processing of data, including annotation, labelling, cleaning, enrichment, and aggregation, where relevant.

rule.hazard: insufficient or unsuitable training data
conditions: If you plan to use existing data sets to train the system
requirements: org shall assess the quantity and suitability of available data sets that will be needed by the system in relation to the data requirements defined in A4.1.

rule.hazard: undocumented dataset assessment leading to unsuitable training data
conditions: If you plan to use existing data sets to train the system
requirements: org shall document the assessment of quantity and suitability of available data sets in the Impact Assessment.

rule.hazard: use of non-compliant data
conditions: against the requirements defined in A4.1
requirements: Define and document methods for evaluating data to be used by the system against the requirements defined in A4.1.

rule.hazard: use of non-compliant data
conditions: using the methods defined in requirement A4.4
requirements: Evaluate all data sets using the methods defined in requirement A4.4.

rule.hazard: use of non-compliant data
conditions: evaluation performed per requirement A4.4
requirements: Document the results of the evaluation.

rule.hazard: unidentified responsible stakeholders
conditions: during and after deployment
requirements: Identify the stakeholders who are responsible for troubleshooting, managing, operating, overseeing, and controlling the system.

rule.hazard: unidentified responsible stakeholders
conditions: during and after deployment
requirements: Document these stakeholders and their oversight and control responsibilities using the Impact Assessment template.

rule.hazard: failure of human oversight due to unidentified system elements
conditions: stakeholders identified in requirement A5.1
requirements: org shall identify the system elements (including system UX, features, alerting and reporting functions, and educational materials) necessary for stakeholders identified in requirement A5.1 to effectively understand their oversight responsibilities and carry them out.

rule.hazard: misuse or incorrect application due to misunderstanding of intended uses
conditions: stakeholders identified in requirement A5.1
requirements: org shall ensure stakeholders identified in requirement A5.1 are able to understand the system's intended uses.

rule.hazard: inadequate human interaction or misinterpretation of system outputs
conditions: stakeholders identified in requirement A5.1
requirements: org shall ensure stakeholders identified in requirement A5.1 are able to understand how to effectively execute interactions with the system and how to interpret system behavior.

rule.hazard: failure to intervene when necessary
conditions: stakeholders identified in requirement A5.1
requirements: org shall ensure stakeholders identified in requirement A5.1 are able to understand when and how to override, intervene, or interrupt the system.

rule.hazard: automation bias leading to over-reliance on system outputs
conditions: stakeholders identified in requirement A5.1
requirements: org shall ensure stakeholders identified in requirement A5.1 are able to remain aware of the possible tendency of over-relying on outputs produced by the system ("automation bias").

rule.hazard: lack of documented support for oversight and control functions
conditions: for each oversight and control function
relevant stakeholders identified in requirement A5.1
requirements: org shall document the system design elements that will support relevant stakeholders for each oversight and control function.

rule.hazard: inability to assess oversight/control effectiveness
requirements: The org shall define and document the method to evaluate whether each oversight or control function can be accomplished by stakeholders in realistic conditions of system use.

rule.hazard: lack of evaluative criteria for oversight functions
requirements: The org shall include the metrics or rubrics that will be used in the evaluations of oversight or control functions.

rule.hazard: insufficient human oversight implementation
conditions: when design of the system elements identified in A5.2 is not possible
requirements: The org shall provide guidance on human oversight considerations to the third party responsible for implementing the system elements identified in A5.2 when design of those elements is not possible.

rule.hazard: undetected issues at release
conditions: evaluations defined by requirement A5.4
using a near-release version of the system
requirements: developer shall conduct the evaluations defined by requirement A5.4 using a near-release version of the system.

rule.hazard: inadequate release safeguards
requirements: org shall define Responsible Release Criteria.

rule.hazard: lack of documented evaluation evidence
conditions: using a near-release version of the system
evaluations defined by requirement A5.4
requirements: developer shall document the results of the evaluations.

rule.hazard: inappropriate system release
requirements: org shall document the Responsible Release Criteria.

rule.hazard: insufficient oversight evaluation by third parties
conditions: when this is not possible (for example, when Microsoft is not responsible for oversight and control functions)
requirements: org shall provide guidance on evaluating oversight and control functions to the third party responsible for evaluating oversight or control functions.

rule.hazard: release with unmet Responsible Release Criteria
conditions: Responsible Release Criteria for metrics or rubrics have not been met
requirements: Consult with the reviewers named in the Impact Assessment to develop a plan detailing how the gap will be managed until it can be closed.

rule.hazard: release with unmet Responsible Release Criteria
conditions: Responsible Release Criteria for metrics or rubrics have not been met
Sensitive Uses
requirements: Consult with the Office of Responsible AI to develop a plan detailing how the gap will be managed until it can be closed.

rule.hazard: release with unmet Responsible Release Criteria
conditions: Responsible Release Criteria for metrics or rubrics have not been met
requirements: Document the plan describing how the gap will be managed until it can be closed.

rule.hazard: decision-making without stakeholder consideration
conditions: when the intended use of the generated outputs is to inform decision making by or about people
requirements: org shall identify stakeholders who will use the outputs of the system to make decisions.

rule.hazard: decision-making without stakeholder consideration
conditions: when the intended use of the generated outputs is to inform decision making by or about people
requirements: org shall identify stakeholders who are subject to decisions informed by the system.

rule.hazard: decision-making without stakeholder consideration
conditions: when the intended use of the generated outputs is to inform decision making by or about people
requirements: org shall document the identified stakeholders using the Impact Assessment template.

rule.hazard: decisions based on misunderstanding of system intended uses
conditions: stakeholders identified in requirement T1.1
requirements: Design the system, including, when possible, the system UX, features, reporting functions, and educational materials, so that stakeholders identified in requirement T1.1 can understand the system's intended uses.

rule.hazard: incorrect decisions due to misinterpretation of system behavior
conditions: stakeholders identified in requirement T1.1
requirements: Design the system so that stakeholders identified in requirement T1.1 can interpret relevant system behavior effectively in a way that supports informed decision making.

rule.hazard: automation bias leading to over-reliance on system outputs
conditions: stakeholders identified in requirement T1.1
requirements: Design the system so that stakeholders identified in requirement T1.1 remain aware of the possible tendency to over-rely on outputs produced by the system ("automation bias").

rule.hazard: stakeholders lack documented guidance to understand and interpret system outputs
conditions: for the two categories of stakeholders identified in requirement T1.1
requirements: Document how the system design will support the two categories of stakeholders' understanding of the system's intended uses.

rule.hazard: stakeholders lack documented guidance to understand and interpret system outputs
conditions: for the two categories of stakeholders identified in requirement T1.1
requirements: Document how the system design aids the two categories of stakeholders' ability to interpret relevant system responses.

rule.hazard: stakeholders lack documented guidance to understand and interpret system outputs
conditions: for the two categories of stakeholders identified in requirement T1.1
requirements: Document how the system design discourages automation bias for the two categories of stakeholders.

rule.hazard: stakeholders making decisions without being evaluated as able to interpret system responses
conditions: each stakeholder who will make decisions or be subject to decisions based on the behavior of the system
requirements: Define and document the method to be used to evaluate whether each stakeholder who will make decisions or be subject to decisions based on the behavior of the system can interpret the relevant system responses reasonably well.

rule.hazard: insufficient evaluation metrics for release decisions
requirements: org shall include the metrics or rubrics that will be used in the evaluations.

rule.hazard: insufficient evaluation metrics for release decisions
requirements: org shall define and document a Responsible Release Plan that includes Responsible Release Criteria to achieve this Goal.

rule.hazard: action based on unverified system behavior
requirements: developer shall conduct the evaluations defined by requirement T1.3.

rule.hazard: action based on unverified system behavior
conditions: pre-release
requirements: developer shall document the pre-release results of the evaluations.

rule.hazard: action based on unverified system behavior
conditions: for ongoing evaluation to continue supporting this Goal
requirements: developer shall determine and document how often ongoing evaluation should be conducted to continue supporting this Goal.

rule.hazard: release with unmet Responsible Release Criteria
conditions: Responsible Release Criteria for metrics or rubrics not met
requirements: org shall consult with the reviewers named in the Impact Assessment.

rule.hazard: release with unmet Responsible Release Criteria
conditions: Responsible Release Criteria for metrics or rubrics not met
requirements: org shall develop a plan detailing how the gap in Responsible Release Criteria for metrics or rubrics will be managed until it can be closed.

rule.hazard: release with unmet Responsible Release Criteria
conditions: Responsible Release Criteria for metrics or rubrics not met
requirements: org shall document the plan for managing the gap until it is closed.

rule.hazard: release with unmet Responsible Release Criteria
conditions: Responsible Release Criteria for metrics or rubrics not met
Sensitive Uses
requirements: org shall consult with the Office of Responsible AI to develop the plan when the uses are Sensitive Uses.

rule.hazard: uninformed decisions about system use
conditions: Applies to: All AI systems.
requirements: Identify and list stakeholders who make decisions about whether to employ the system for particular tasks.

rule.hazard: uninformed decisions about system use
conditions: Applies to: All AI systems.
requirements: Identify and list stakeholders who develop or deploy systems that integrate with this system.

rule.hazard: uninformed decisions about system use
conditions: Applies to: All AI systems.
requirements: Document the identified stakeholders in the Impact Assessment template.

rule.hazard: stakeholder misunderstanding
requirements: org must publish documentation for the system that enables the stakeholders defined in T2.1 to understand the system and that includes the system's capabilities and intended uses.

rule.hazard: stakeholder misunderstanding
requirements: org must publish documentation that includes uses that require extra care or guidance.

rule.hazard: stakeholder misunderstanding
requirements: org must publish documentation that describes operational factors and settings that allow for effective and responsible system use.

rule.hazard: stakeholder misunderstanding
requirements: org must publish documentation that describes the system's limitations, including uses for which the system was not designed or evaluated.

rule.hazard: stakeholder misunderstanding
requirements: org must publish documentation that provides evidence of system accuracy and performance and describes the extent to which these results are generalizable across use cases not part of the evaluation.

rule.hazard: stakeholder misunderstanding
conditions: system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners
requirements: org must publish a Transparency Note when the system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners.

rule.hazard: outdated documentation
conditions: annually
requirements: org shall review and update documentation annually.

rule.hazard: outdated documentation
conditions: new uses are added
functionality changes
the product moves to a new release stage
new information about reliable and safe performance becomes known as defined by requirement RS3.3
new information about system accuracy and performance becomes available
requirements: org shall review and update documentation when any condition in conditions[] occurs.

rule.hazard: stakeholders lack required transparency information
conditions: when the system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners
requirements: org shall include this information in the required Transparency Note when the system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners.

rule.hazard: user deception via unlabeled AI interaction
conditions: system impersonates interactions with humans
exceptions: obvious from the circumstances or context of use that an AI system is in use
requirements: Developer shall design the AI system to inform people that they are interacting with an AI system.

rule.hazard: inadequate impact assessment due to unidentified stakeholders
requirements: Org shall identify stakeholders who will use or be exposed to the system in accordance with the Impact Assessment requirements.

rule.hazard: unlabeled AI-generated outputs
conditions: outputs intended to be used outside the system
requirements: Deployer shall label any image, audio, or video outputs that are intended to be used outside the system as being produced by AI.

rule.hazard: stakeholders not informed of AI system type
conditions: stakeholders identified in T3.1
requirements: Developer shall design the system UX, features, reporting functions, educational materials, and outputs to inform stakeholders identified in T3.1 of the type of AI system they are interacting with or exposed to.

rule.hazard: stakeholders not documented in impact assessment
requirements: Org shall document these stakeholders using the Impact Assessment template.

rule.hazard: stakeholders uninformed about AI system type
conditions: Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoint
for each stakeholder identified in T3.1
evaluating whether the stakeholder is informed of the type of AI system they are interacting with or exposed to
requirements: The org shall define and document the method to be used to evaluate whether each stakeholder identified in T3.1 is informed of the type of AI system they are interacting with or exposed to.

rule.hazard: stakeholders uninformed about AI system type
conditions: Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoint
to achieve this Goal
requirements: The org shall define and document Responsible Release Criteria to achieve this Goal.

rule.hazard: failure to perform required evaluations
requirements: developer shall conduct the evaluations defined by requirement T3.3.

rule.hazard: release without documented pre-release evaluation results
requirements: developer shall document the pre-release results of the evaluations.

rule.hazard: insufficient ongoing evaluation frequency
requirements: developer shall determine and document how often ongoing evaluation should be conducted to continue supporting this goal.

rule.hazard: unequal quality of service across demographic groups
conditions: based on intended uses and geographic areas where the system will be deployed
requirements: Identify demographic groups, including marginalized groups, that may be at risk of experiencing worse quality of service.

rule.hazard: unequal quality of service across demographic groups
conditions: based on intended uses and geographic areas where the system will be deployed
requirements: Prioritize the identified demographic groups for remediation or mitigation of worse quality of service.

rule.hazard: unequal quality of service across demographic groups
conditions: based on intended uses and geographic areas where the system will be deployed
requirements: Include groups defined by a single factor in the identification and prioritization process.

rule.hazard: unequal quality of service across demographic groups
conditions: based on intended uses and geographic areas where the system will be deployed
requirements: Include groups defined by a combination of factors in the identification and prioritization process.

rule.hazard: underrepresentation of demographic groups
requirements: developer shall evaluate all data sets to assess inclusiveness of the identified demographic groups.

rule.hazard: underrepresentation of demographic groups
requirements: developer shall collect data to close identified inclusiveness gaps.

rule.hazard: underrepresentation of demographic groups
requirements: developer shall document the prioritized identified demographic groups, the evaluation process, and its results using the Impact Assessment template.

rule.hazard: performance disparity between groups
requirements: org shall document the evaluation to be performed to support this Goal.

rule.hazard: performance disparity between groups
requirements: org shall document any system components to be evaluated, in addition to the whole system.

rule.hazard: performance disparity between groups
requirements: org shall document the metrics to be used to evaluate the system components and the whole system.

rule.hazard: performance disparity between groups
requirements: org shall document a description of the data set to be used for this evaluation.

rule.hazard: performance disparity between groups
requirements: For each metric, org shall document any target minimum performance level that must be met for all groups.

rule.hazard: performance disparity between groups
requirements: For each metric, org shall document the target maximum (absolute or relative) performance difference allowed between groups.

rule.hazard: performance disparities across demographic groups
requirements: developer shall evaluate the system according to the defined Responsible Release Criteria.

rule.hazard: low performance for an identified demographic group
conditions: identified demographic group does not meet any target minimum performance level
requirements: developer shall reassess the system design, including the choice of training data, features, objective function, and training algorithm, to improve performance for any identified demographic group that does not meet any target minimum performance level.

rule.hazard: performance differences between demographic groups
conditions: identified demographic groups exceed the target maximum
requirements: developer shall reassess the system design, including the choice of training data, features, objective function, and training algorithm, to minimize performance differences between identified demographic groups, with particular attention to groups that exceed the target maximum.

rule.hazard: legal noncompliance
requirements: org shall consult with its attorney to determine its approach, including how it will identify and document.

rule.hazard: performance disparities between demographic groups
conditions: Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoint
any inability to meet any target minimum performance level for any identified demographic group
requirements: Developer shall identify and document any justifiable factors (e.g., "background noise" for speech recognition systems or "image resolution" for facial recognition systems) that account for any inability to meet any target minimum performance level for any identified demographic group.

rule.hazard: performance disparities between demographic groups
conditions: Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoint
any remaining performance differences between identified demographic groups
requirements: Developer shall identify and document any justifiable factors (e.g., "background noise" for speech recognition systems or "image resolution" for facial recognition systems) that account for any remaining performance differences between identified demographic groups.

rule.hazard: lack of documented pre-release evaluation results
conditions: Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoint
pre-release results from requirements F1.4, F1.5, and F1.6
requirements: Developer shall document the pre-release results from requirements F1.4, F1.5, and F1.6.

rule.hazard: undetected performance degradation
requirements: org shall determine how often ongoing evaluation should be conducted to continue supporting this Goal.

rule.hazard: undetected performance degradation
requirements: org shall document how often ongoing evaluation will be conducted to continue supporting this Goal.

rule.hazard: undisclosed performance disparities
requirements: org shall publish for customers any remaining performance disparities between identified demographic groups that may exceed the target maximum.

rule.hazard: undisclosed performance disparities
requirements: org shall publish for customers any justifiable factors that account for these performance levels and differences.

rule.hazard: undisclosed performance disparities
conditions: system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners
requirements: provider shall include the information described in F1.9 (items 1–3) in the required Transparency Note.

rule.hazard: disparities in outcomes for demographic groups
conditions: based on intended uses
based on geographic areas where the system will be deployed
requirements: The org must identify and prioritize demographic groups, including marginalized groups, that may be at risk of being differentially impacted by the system.

rule.hazard: disparities in outcomes for demographic groups
conditions: based on intended uses
based on geographic areas where the system will be deployed
when identifying and prioritizing demographic groups
requirements: The org must include groups defined by a single factor when identifying and prioritizing demographic groups that may be at risk of differential impact.

rule.hazard: disparities in outcomes for demographic groups
conditions: based on intended uses
based on geographic areas where the system will be deployed
when identifying and prioritizing demographic groups
requirements: The org must include groups defined by a combination of factors when identifying and prioritizing demographic groups that may be at risk of differential impact.

rule.hazard: underrepresentation of demographic groups
requirements: org shall document the prioritized identified demographic groups using the Impact Assessment template.

rule.hazard: underrepresentation of demographic groups
requirements: org shall evaluate all data sets to assess inclusiveness of the identified demographic groups.

rule.hazard: underrepresentation of demographic groups
requirements: org shall collect additional data to close any gaps in inclusiveness of the identified demographic groups.

rule.hazard: underrepresentation of demographic groups
requirements: org shall document the evaluation process and its results.

rule.hazard: disparate allocation of resources and opportunities between groups
conditions: for each metric
requirements: Org shall document, for each metric, the target maximum difference (absolute or relative) between the rates at which resources and opportunities are allocated to groups.

rule.hazard: insufficiently specified release criteria leading to inconsistent release decisions
requirements: Org shall define and document Responsible Release Criteria to achieve this Goal.

rule.hazard: undocumented evaluation scope causing incomplete assessments
requirements: Org shall define and document the evaluation that will be performed to support this Goal.

rule.hazard: omitting system components from evaluation
requirements: Org shall document any system components to be evaluated, in addition to the whole system.

rule.hazard: using unspecified metrics for evaluation
requirements: Org shall document the metrics to be used to evaluate the system components and the whole system.

rule.hazard: evaluation based on undocumented data
requirements: Org shall document the data set to be used for this evaluation.

rule.hazard: disparate allocation of resources and opportunities across demographic groups
conditions: according to the defined Responsible Release Criteria
requirements: Developer shall evaluate the system according to the defined Responsible Release Criteria.

rule.hazard: disparate allocation of resources and opportunities across demographic groups
requirements: Developer shall reassess the system design, including the choice of training data, features, objective function, and training algorithm, to pursue the goal of minimizing differences between the rates at which resources and opportunities are allocated to identified demographic groups.

rule.hazard: disparate allocation of resources and opportunities across demographic groups
requirements: Developer shall pay particular attention to demographic groups that exceed the target maximum difference when performing the reassessment.

rule.hazard: legal non-compliance
requirements: The org must consult with its attorney to determine its approach to this.

rule.hazard: legal non-compliance
requirements: The org must determine how it will identify and document trade-offs.

rule.hazard: unjustified disparity in allocation of resources and opportunities between demographic groups
conditions: remaining differences in allocation rates between identified demographic groups
requirements: org shall identify and document any justifiable factors that account for any remaining differences between the rates at which resources and opportunities are allocated to identified demographic groups.

rule.hazard: unjustified disparity in allocation of resources and opportunities between demographic groups
conditions: pre-release results for the evaluation described by requirements F2.4, F2.5, and F2.6
requirements: org shall document the pre-release results for the evaluation described by requirements F2.4, F2.5, and F2.6.

rule.hazard: insufficient ongoing evaluation frequency leading to undetected allocation disparities
conditions: ongoing evaluation to continue supporting this goal
requirements: org shall determine and document how often ongoing evaluation should be conducted to continue supporting this goal.

rule.hazard: undisclosed allocation disparities
requirements: Org shall publish for customers any remaining differences between the rates at which resources and opportunities are allocated to identified demographic groups.

rule.hazard: undisclosed allocation disparities
requirements: Org shall publish for customers any justifiable factors that account for the remaining differences in allocation rates between identified demographic groups.

rule.hazard: undisclosed allocation disparities
conditions: system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners
requirements: When the system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners, org shall include this information in the required Transparency Note.

rule.hazard: stereotyping, demeaning, or erasing of demographic groups
requirements: developer shall identify and prioritize demographic groups, including marginalized groups, that may be at risk of being subject to stereotyping, demeaning, or erasing outputs of the system.

rule.hazard: stereotyping, demeaning, or erasing of demographic groups
requirements: developer shall include groups defined by a single factor in the identified and prioritized demographic groups.

rule.hazard: stereotyping, demeaning, or erasing of demographic groups
requirements: developer shall include groups defined by a combination of factors in the identified and prioritized demographic groups.

rule.hazard: stereotyping, demeaning, and erasing of prioritized identified demographic groups
requirements: Document a plan to evaluate the system components and the whole system for risks of stereotyping, demeaning, and erasing the prioritized identified demographic groups in the Impact Assessment template.

rule.hazard: stereotyping, demeaning, and erasing of prioritized identified demographic groups
requirements: Document the prioritized identified demographic groups using the Impact Assessment template.

rule.hazard: stereotyping, demeaning, and erasing of prioritized identified demographic groups
requirements: Define and document any system components to be evaluated, in addition to the whole system, in the Impact Assessment template.

rule.hazard: stereotyping of identified demographic groups
conditions: according to the plan defined in requirement F3.3
Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoint
requirements: Developer shall evaluate the system according to the plan defined in requirement F3.3.

rule.hazard: stereotyping of identified demographic groups
conditions: Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoint
requirements: Developer shall reassess the system design, including the choice of training data, features, objective function, and training algorithm, to pursue the goal of minimizing the potential for stereotyping of the identified demographic groups.

rule.hazard: undetected regressions in fairness (stereotyping/demeaning/erasing)
requirements: org shall document the pre-release results from requirements F3.4 and F3.5.

rule.hazard: undetected regressions in fairness (stereotyping/demeaning/erasing)
requirements: org shall determine and document how often ongoing evaluation should be conducted to continue supporting this goal.

rule.hazard: risks involving identified demographic groups
requirements: org shall publish information for customers about these risks involving identified demographic groups.

rule.hazard: risks involving identified demographic groups
conditions: when the system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners
requirements: org shall include this information in the required Transparency Note.

rule.hazard: unsafe or unreliable system behavior
conditions: Applies to: All AI systems
Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoint
requirements: The org shall document how reliable and safe behavior is defined for this system.

rule.hazard: unsafe or unreliable system behavior
conditions: Applies to: All AI systems
Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoint
in the context of intended uses
requirements: The org shall document what acceptable error rates are for overall system performance in the context of intended uses.

rule.hazard: unsafe system behavior due to unrepresentative training or test data
requirements: developer shall evaluate training and test data sets to ensure they include representation of the intended uses, operational factors, and an appropriate range of settings for each factor.

rule.hazard: loss of traceability for evaluation decisions
requirements: developer shall document the evaluation of the training and test data sets.

rule.hazard: unsafe system behavior due to unmanaged operational factors
requirements: developer shall determine the operational factors, including quality of system input, use, and operational context, that are critical to manage for reliable and safe use of the system in its deployed context.

rule.hazard: lack of documented operational requirements for safe deployment
requirements: developer shall document the determined operational factors and their relevance to reliable and safe use in the system's deployed context.

rule.hazard: unsafe or unreliable system operation
requirements: Developer shall define and document acceptable ranges for each operational factor important to support reliable and safe system use.

rule.hazard: unsafe or unreliable system operation
requirements: Developer shall define and document an acceptable error rate for the system when operating within the defined operational factor ranges.

rule.hazard: excessive error rates (false positives/false negatives)
conditions: where additional operational factors, more narrow or different acceptable ranges, or lower acceptable error rates are advised
requirements: The org shall define intended uses, if any, where additional operational factors, more narrow or different acceptable ranges, or lower acceptable error rates (including false positive and false negative error rates) are advised to ensure reliability and safety.

rule.hazard: excessive error rates (false positives/false negatives)
conditions: after defining intended uses (RS1.5)
requirements: The org shall document its conclusions from the intended-use definition.

rule.hazard: excessive error rates (false positives/false negatives)
conditions: based on requirements RS1.1, RS1.3, RS1.4, and RS1.5
requirements: The org shall define an evaluation plan based on requirements RS1.1, RS1.3, RS1.4, and RS1.5.

rule.hazard: excessive error rates (false positives/false negatives)
requirements: The org shall document the evaluation plan and include the environment in which the system will be evaluated.

rule.hazard: unsafe or unreliable system behavior
requirements: developer shall evaluate the system according to the evaluation plan defined in requirement RS1.6 to ensure reliable and safe system behavior.

rule.hazard: unsafe or unreliable system behavior
requirements: developer shall document the pre-release results of the evaluation.

rule.hazard: unsafe or unreliable system behavior
requirements: developer shall determine and document how often ongoing evaluation should be conducted to continue supporting this goal.

rule.hazard: reliability degradation
conditions: failure cases within operational factors and defined ranges
requirements: deployer shall resolve issues arising from failure cases within operational factors and defined ranges.

rule.hazard: deployment despite unmet release criteria
conditions: Responsible Release Criteria RS1.1, RS1.3, RS1.4, and RS1.5 cannot be met
requirements: org shall reassess intended uses when Responsible Release Criteria RS1.1, RS1.3, RS1.4, and RS1.5 cannot be met.

rule.hazard: deployment despite unmet release criteria
conditions: Responsible Release Criteria RS1.1, RS1.3, RS1.4, and RS1.5 cannot be met
requirements: org shall update documentation when Responsible Release Criteria RS1.1, RS1.3, RS1.4, and RS1.5 cannot be met.

rule.hazard: use in unsupported contexts
requirements: org shall provide documentation to customers and potential customers that includes the outputs of requirements RS1.2, RS1.7 and RS1.8.

rule.hazard: use in unsupported contexts
requirements: org shall include any unsupported uses defined in the Impact Assessment and in RS1.8 in the documentation provided to customers and potential customers.

rule.hazard: use in unsupported contexts
conditions: system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners
requirements: When the system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners, org shall include the information required above in the documentation available to those external customers or partners.

rule.hazard: delayed remediation of predictable or known failures
conditions: Applies to: All AI systems.
requirements: Design the AI system to minimize the time to remediation of predictable or known failures.

rule.hazard: unaddressed false positive or false negative failures
conditions: Applies to: All AI systems.
requirements: Define predictable failures, including false positive and false negative results, for the system as a whole.

rule.hazard: stakeholder harm from predictable failures
conditions: Applies to: All AI systems.
for each intended use
requirements: Document how predictable failures would impact stakeholders for each intended use.

rule.hazard: undocumented adverse impacts of predictable failures on stakeholders
conditions: Applies to: All AI systems.
for predictable or known failures
requirements: Use the Impact Assessment template to document any adverse impacts of these failures on stakeholders.

rule.hazard: adverse stakeholder impact from predictable failures
conditions: for each case of a predictable failure likely to have an adverse impact on a stakeholder
when possible
requirements: Design and build the system to avoid the predictable failure.

rule.hazard: adverse stakeholder impact from predictable failures
conditions: for each case of a predictable failure likely to have an adverse impact on a stakeholder
when a failure cannot be prevented by design
requirements: Build a fallback option that can be used when the predictable failure occurs.

rule.hazard: adverse stakeholder impact from predictable failures
conditions: for each case of a predictable failure likely to have an adverse impact on a stakeholder
requirements: Document the failure management approach for each case of a predictable failure likely to have an adverse impact on a stakeholder.

rule.hazard: adverse stakeholder impact from predictable failures
conditions: for each case of a predictable failure likely to have an adverse impact on a stakeholder
when possible
requirements: Describe the design solution that is used to avoid the predictable failure.

rule.hazard: adverse stakeholder impact from predictable failures
conditions: for each case of a predictable failure likely to have an adverse impact on a stakeholder
for each designed solution
when possible
requirements: Estimate the time range for resolving the predictable failure for each designed solution, or indicate that the failure will be prevented by design.

rule.hazard: inability to invoke fallback causing prolonged outage
requirements: org shall describe the fallback option and document the estimated time required to invoke and use the fallback option.

rule.hazard: failures not remediated due to untrained stakeholders
conditions: predictable failures identified in requirement RS2.1
requirements: org shall provide training and documentation for stakeholders accountable for system oversight, system owners, developers, customer support, and other stakeholders to support their remediation and mitigation of failures.

rule.hazard: inability to rollback across endpoints causing persistent failure
conditions: previously unknown failures that come to light once the system is in use
requirements: org shall describe the system's rollback plan and document the time that may elapse until the entire system, across all endpoints, can be rolled back.

rule.hazard: inability to disable feature across endpoints prolonging failure
conditions: previously unknown failures that come to light once the system is in use
requirements: org shall describe support for turning features off and document the time that may elapse until the feature can be turned off across all endpoints.

rule.hazard: delayed model updates across endpoints
conditions: previously unknown failures that come to light once the system is in use
requirements: org shall describe the process for updating and releasing updates to each model and document the time that may elapse until the system has been updated across all endpoints.

rule.hazard: stakeholders not notified of failures and mitigations
conditions: previously unknown failures that come to light once the system is in use
requirements: org shall describe how customers, partners, and end users will be notified of changes to the system, updated understandings of failures, and their best mitigations.

rule.hazard: undetected failure modes
requirements: developer shall conduct a Failure Mode and Effects Analysis.

rule.hazard: poor human-AI interaction
conditions: when designing the system
requirements: developer shall follow the Guidelines for Human-AI Interaction when designing the system.

rule.hazard: insufficient documentation
requirements: org shall document the training and documentation provided.

rule.hazard: undetected system failures
requirements: org shall subject all AI systems to ongoing monitoring, feedback, and evaluation to identify new uses, troubleshoot issues, manage and maintain the systems, and improve them over time.

rule.hazard: missing monitoring data or feedback channels
requirements: org shall establish and document a detailed inventory of the system health monitoring methods to be used, including data and insights generated from data repositories, system analytics, and associated alerts; processes by which customers can submit information about failures and concerns; and processes by which the general public can submit feedback.

rule.hazard: unresolved system failures
requirements: org shall define and document a standard operating procedure and a system health monitoring action plan for each monitoring channel, including processes for reproducing system failures to support troubleshooting and prevention of future failures; which events will be monitored; how events will be prioritized for review and for response and timing to resolution; the expected frequency of reviews; escalation of high priority issues to the Office of Responsible AI; and engagement of customer service to ensure they are aware of how to respond to issues for the system.

rule.hazard: unreliable or unsafe system performance due to unsupported new uses or operational factors
conditions: When new uses, critical operational factors, or changes in the supported range of an operational factor are identified
requirements: Determine whether any new use or operational factor can be supported with the existing system, will be supported but require additional work, or will not be supported.

rule.hazard: unreliable or unsafe system performance due to unsupported new uses or operational factors
conditions: When new uses or operational factors identified are to be supported
requirements: Evaluate the updated system in accordance with requirement RS1.6.

rule.hazard: unreliable or unsafe system performance due to unsupported new uses or operational factors
conditions: When new uses or operational factors identified are to be supported
requirements: Add the new intended use to the Impact Assessment.

rule.hazard: unreliable or unsafe system performance due to unsupported new uses or operational factors
conditions: When new uses or operational factors identified are to be supported
requirements: Publish updated customer communication in accordance with requirement RS1.9.

rule.hazard: unreliable or unsafe system performance due to unsupported new uses or operational factors
conditions: When these new uses or operational factor range changes cannot or will not be accommodated to ensure reliable and safe performance of the system
When the system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners
requirements: Update customer documentation described in RS1.9 to include the new use as an unsupported use.

rule.hazard: unreliable or unsafe system performance due to unsupported new uses or operational factors
conditions: When these new uses or operational factor range changes cannot or will not be accommodated to ensure reliable and safe performance of the system
When the system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners
requirements: If the system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners, include this information in the required Transparency Note.

rule.hazard: unsafe or unqualified operation when used for Sensitive Use beyond intended ranges
conditions: When a system is to be used for a Sensitive Use that imposes qualification or quality control requirements beyond the intended uses and/or operational factor ranges
requirements: Conduct an evaluation specific to the Sensitive Use.

rule.hazard: release despite unmet Responsible Release Criteria
conditions: required Responsible Release Criteria cannot be met
requirements: org shall review the results and decide how to proceed when the required Responsible Release Criteria cannot be met.

rule.hazard: undocumented Responsible Release Criteria changes
requirements: org shall document any changes to the Responsible Release Criteria.

rule.hazard: unrecorded evaluation results
requirements: org shall document the results of evaluation.

rule.hazard: omission of required ongoing evaluations
conditions: evaluations tagged as Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoints in other Goals
requirements: org shall conduct all evaluations tagged as Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoints in other Goals on an ongoing basis.

rule.hazard: continued operation with unmet evaluation targets
conditions: targets in Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoints that are no longer satisfied
requirements: consult with named reviewers

rule.hazard: continued operation with unmet evaluation targets
conditions: targets in Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoints that are no longer satisfied
requirements: in the case of Sensitive Uses consult with the Office of Responsible AI to develop and implement a plan to close any gaps

rule.hazard: continued operation with unmet evaluation targets
conditions: targets in Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoints that are no longer satisfied
requirements: develop and implement a plan to close any gaps

rule.hazard: lack of records about corrective actions taken after evaluation failures
conditions: targets in Ongoing Evaluation Checkpoints that are no longer satisfied
requirements: document the process, its results, and conclusions

rule.hazard: continued operation despite evidence the system is not fit for an intended use
conditions: evidence comes to light that refutes the system is fit for purpose for an intended use at any point in the system's use
requirements: remove the intended use from customer-facing materials

rule.hazard: continued operation despite evidence the system is not fit for an intended use
conditions: evidence comes to light that refutes the system is fit for purpose for an intended use at any point in the system's use
requirements: make current customers aware of the issue

rule.hazard: continued operation despite evidence the system is not fit for an intended use
conditions: evidence comes to light that refutes the system is fit for purpose for an intended use at any point in the system's use
requirements: take action to close the identified gap or discontinue the system

rule.hazard: customers using outdated or incorrect documentation after loss of fitness for purpose
conditions: evidence comes to light that refutes the system is fit for purpose for an intended use at any point in the system's use
requirements: revise documentation related to the intended use

rule.hazard: customers using outdated or incorrect documentation after loss of fitness for purpose
conditions: evidence comes to light that refutes the system is fit for purpose for an intended use at any point in the system's use
requirements: publish the revised documentation to customers

rule.hazard: failure to transparently inform external customers/partners about loss of fitness for purpose
conditions: evidence comes to light that refutes the system is fit for purpose for an intended use at any point in the system's use
system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners
requirements: include this information in the required Transparency Note

rule.hazard: outdated transparency documentation
conditions: new uses are added
functionality changes
new information about reliable and safe performance becomes known as defined by requirement RS3.3
new information about system accuracy and performance becomes available
requirements: The org shall review and update the documentation required by Goal T2.

rule.hazard: missing transparency information for external customers or partners
conditions: system is a platform service made available to external customers or partners
requirements: The org shall include this information in the required Transparency Note.

rule.hazard: unresolved non-compliance with the Standard
conditions: unresolved issue related to supporting the Standard and its requirements
requirements: The org shall escalate unresolved issues related to supporting the Standard and its requirements to the Office of Responsible AI.

rule.hazard: unauthorized disclosure of personal data
conditions: Applies when: Microsoft Privacy Standard applies.
requirements: Design AI systems to protect privacy in accordance with the Microsoft Privacy Standard.

rule.hazard: security breach
conditions: Applies when: Microsoft Security Policy applies.
requirements: Design AI systems to be secure in accordance with the Microsoft Security Policy.

rule.hazard: inaccessible AI system
conditions: Microsoft Accessibility Standards apply
requirements: The developer must design AI systems to be inclusive in accordance with the Microsoft Accessibility Standards.
